frameworks.name: sensory prediction error
frameworks.one_line: dopamine signals errors about identity of sensory events, not just value

frameworks.name: associative learning
frameworks.one_line: dopamine transients are taken to support stimulus-stimulus learning

frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: content of error is read out from across-neuron firing pattern

frameworks.name: scalar reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: single-neuron/population-average view cannot carry identity information

core_question: Does the dopamine system signal not just that a sensory prediction was violated but what was mis-predicted?
key_assumptions: dopamine transients can support value-neutral, sensory-based associative learning
downstream learners need content-specific teaching signals
information not visible in averages may live in ensemble patterns
manipulations: violations of expected sensory event identity (value-neutral)
rat single-unit recording vs human midbrain BOLD comparison
key_findings: individual dopamine neurons and population averages do not show identity-specific responses
across-cell firing pattern in rat dopamine neurons discriminates which sensory event was mis-predicted
human midbrain BOLD response to sensory prediction errors carries analogous identity information
interpretations: dopamine ensembles carry multidimensional, content-specific teaching signals
downstream regions can read identity of prediction error from the dopamine population code
